feed.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge

entry.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge
entry.summary:
The recent implementation of the suggested variants in cryptographic proof mechanisms has yielded some notable results, particularly with respect to efficiency and resource utilization. The implementation includes two variants: a full Taproot image and a hardened xpriv image, each tested under composite and succinct conditions. In the case of the full Taproot image, while the succinct proof did not offer significant size reductions, there was a noticeable improvement in the final proof time when compared to previous tests, highlighting the need for an enhanced benchmark environment.
For the hardened xpriv variant, results were more pronounced. The composite seal required significantly less memory and proved considerably faster than its full Taproot counterpart, taking only about 2 seconds on the test machine and utilizing just 3 GB of memory for the proof. This variant not only demonstrated a rapid proving time but also maintained a low memory usage, suggesting a substantial enhancement in performance efficiency.
Further resources have been developed to provide detailed documentation on these new proof variants and their results. These include a series of documents accessible online which explain the technical details and outcomes of the implemented proofs ([reduced variants](https://github.com/Roasbeef/bip32-pq-zkp/blob/main/docs/reduced-variants.md), [proof variants README](https://github.com/Roasbeef/bip32-pq-zkp/blob/1c89fdb398180a2b3eff7761b7f4b233d455c6c9/README.mdreduced-proof-variants), and [claim reduced variant claims](https://github.com/Roasbeef/bip32-pq-zkp/blob/438c548ca9b49d83ef4019974a5171f5e06fa840/docs/claim.mdreduced-variant-claims)). These documents are essential for anyone looking to understand the specifics of the cryptographic improvements made.
This advancement in cryptographic proof development not only reflects the technical progress in terms of efficiency and resource management but also opens up discussions for further improvements, possibly involving the integration of an AIR compiler to delve deeper into optimization possibilities.
